div1.entryFree:
Relish, subst. 1) a pleasing taste: what r. is in this? Tw. IV, 1, 64. the imaginary r. is so sweet, Troil. III, 2, 20. some crab-trees that will not be grafted to your r. Cor. II, 1, 206 (O. Edd. rallish).
2) an admixture just perceptible, a tincture: some smack of age, some r. of the saltness of time, H4B I, 2, 111. the king-becoming graces . . . I have no r. of them, Mcb. IV, 3, 95. some act that has no r. of salvation in it, Hml. III, 3, 92.
3) quality, sort: his fears . . . be of the same r. as ours are, H5 IV, 1, 114.
